set.body:
Goal
- Ship a broader beta where `adgeMatrix` is a practical drop-in Matrix replacement and `mode = "fast"` adapts automatically across installed healthy backends (`mlx`, `opencl`, `arrayfire`, `metal`) with honest CPU fallback.

Exit criteria
- Core Matrix-style workflows succeed under plain `library(amatrix)` without attach-order traps, class demotion, or common indexing/subassignment surprises.
- Installed healthy backends are auto-discovered and eligible for planning without package-author backend-specific code.
- Backend tiers are evidence-backed on fixed hardware and match the README/runtime status surfaces.
- Release gate proves the claim mechanically: backend CI, benchmark regression, fallback-log cleanliness, and tier generation all green.

Tracks
- Re-verify blocker bugs and retire stale reports.
- Core drop-in semantics.
- Adaptive backend routing/discovery.
- Backend certification for MLX, ArrayFire, OpenCL, Metal.
- Release-gate hardening and beta sign-off.
set.title: Broader beta release: drop-in Matrix semantics plus adaptive multi-backend acceleration
